title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Go Semantics Case: Upstream Admission Budget
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must bound in-flight confirms and bytes, consume blocked signals, serialize channel use, and propagate saturation upstream; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upstream admission budget increment in a cancellable Go publisher admission controller. Learners pr…
objectives: Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Upstream Admission Budget · Theory Upstream Admission Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3271 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Upstream Admission Budget contract for the supplied data.
